name: Hajjah
description: The mountainous Haraz region of Hajjah governorate contains Yemen's most dramatic defensive architecture — villages growing directly from cliff faces, with the geological rock forming the rear wall of buildings. Al-Hajjarah and Shibam Kawkaban are the defining examples.
traditional: Building grows from cliff face; local schist stone coursed dry; cisterns carved into bedrock.
transitional: Stone structure stepped up cliff with modern water supply; traditional massing retained.
contemporary: Contemporary Haraz: stone cladding, stepped profile following topography, terrace gardens.
